# Paper 3: Coexistence Without Differentiation

## Context

Papers 1--2 produce individuation and coexistence, but assemblages are
structural clones. Can any mechanism within the Hebbian framework produce
functional differentiation?

## Key Findings

**All interventions fail.** Temporal variation, anti-Hebbian inhibition, and
edge injection all fail across 1,400 simulations.

**Self-sealing.** The substrate seals through topological isolation
(cross-assemblage edges decay within 15 sessions) and functional absorption
(maintained edges are co-opted). Paper 4S showed the timeline is
pruning-accelerated.

## Contribution

The identification of self-sealing as a structural property of Hebbian
individuation, establishing that differenciation requires mechanisms beyond
the basic Hebbian rule.
